pattern: For each prefix ending at i, the answer is the length of the longest non-decreasing subsequence ending there, maintained with a patience-sorting tails array and an upper-bound binary search.
hints: Each position must end the chosen course, so you want the longest non-decreasing run that finishes at that obstacle.
Maintain the classic LIS tails array, but allow equal values so the subsequence may be non-decreasing.
For each height find the first tail strictly greater than it (upper bound); replace it, and the 1-based insertion index is that position's answer.

edge_inputs.description: rise then a dip that still extends to length 3
strictly decreasing keeps every answer at 1
all equal heights grow the course every step

stress.description: long random heights over a moderate value range
stress.note: duplicates from the small range stress the non-decreasing upper-bound logic
